title: SprucePilot CX
preview: Inbound via self-serve signup (used initial free credits). Product-led trial: created API key, ran basic text-gen for ticket replies. CTO (Rina Patel) + Head of Support (Marco Reyes) on initial call. Key asks: fast, structured JSON outputs for triage system; tool-calling to create/update tickets in their internal ticketing microservice; server-side ticket summarization to include "summary", "priority_hint", "possible_tags" fields. Latency: P95 target <= 600ms for agent-assist flows; throughput typical 20-50 req/min, bursts up to 200 req/min during morning surge. Cost sensitivity: high — SMB budget, wants predictable per-request pricing and guidance on batching/kv-caching to reduce token cost. Model preference: open models or Redwood-curated smaller models to control cost; open to Redwood recommendations. Security: requires SSO (SAML), audit logs retention 90 days, KMS for keys. Quote from CTO: "We need clean JSON back so our triage automation can onboard quickly — no brittle parsing steps." AE notes: friendly, technical CTO, pragmatic about tradeoffs. Mentioned evaluating Zendesk AI beta but wants more control and cheaper token economics.

title: Autumn Surge Analytics
preview:
Inbound SMB trial. Fast signup; self-serve. Created first key but usage stalled after smoke test. Key facts:
- Primary contact: Thomas Reed (CTO, small engineering team).
- Use-case: searchable product docs + in-app support assistant. Wants embeddings + chat routing as next step.
- Latency target: 200-350ms p95 for short chat flows; throughput low (10-30 qps peak).
- Cost sensitivity: high — looking to minimize per-token spend; asked about batching + prefix caching.
- Model preference: open models OK if cost/latency competitive; asked about Redwood-verified low-cost variants.
- Security: basic SOC2 assurance acceptable for now; will escalate to SSO/SAML if they move to Dedicated.
Challenges/observations:
- After initial SE call, no real requests beyond healthcheck. Likely cause: team started evaluating OpenAI due to aggressive free trial credits and ecosystem plugins.
- Quote from Thomas on 2026-03-09: "we can’t justify a switch until we see $/q improvement or simpler SDKs — send a sample cost calc".
- They requested a short batching example and a sample cost projection; we sent docs (drive link above) but no follow-up.
Next steps recommended:
- Quick, personalized ROI email showing concrete $/1000-queries comparison with configured batching + caching scenarios.
- Offer a 2-week trial top-up or credit to run embeddings job (their blocker).
- Book 30-min troubleshooting seat with Marcus to help run the first embeddings pipeline.
- If no re-engagement by 2026-03-20, mark as at-risk churn and add to winback cadence.
Stale signals: trial expiry set to auto-expire 2026-03-15 if no activity (system).

title: Copper Fern Digital
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from self-serve signup. Team: 4 engineers, 1 product owner. Primary goal: embed streaming chat into merchant support console with tool-calls to inventory/returns microservices and strict structured JSON for downstream order updates. Initial demo showed rapid latency and token cost wins on hosted API. 

Key pain reported: using 'JSON mode' (prompt-based) they get valid output most of the time, but in streaming mode tokens occasionally include trailing commentary or partial arrays that fail client JSON.parse. They also tried function calling (function_call:auto) but their function signatures differ slightly (optional fields, snake_case vs camelCase) and client SDK errors are bubbled up and confuse front-end handlers.

Customer quote (PM): "When streaming drops a stray "" or newline we immediately blow up our parsers — not acceptable for order update flows."

Dev notes: using Redwood JS SDK v0.9, Node 18. Sample prompt wraps response in explicit JSON markers but streaming still emits prefix text before first '{' intermittently. They have tiny POC dataset and can reproduce ~1/200 conversations. POC wants deterministic strict-json behavior or reliable function_call mode in streaming.

Suggested troubleshooting steps we gave: enable verbose SDK logs, toggle streaming buffer delimiter to '�' sentinel, set function schemas with required fields and exact keys, force function_call with name, and try 'json_mode: strict' on server side. They agreed to run with logging on and share console link.

Sales/ops: interested to stay hosted to avoid infra ops. Cost sensitivity moderate (SMB) — willing to commit if streaming stability proven.

Next meeting: 30m technical triage w/ Priya + Marcus + engineering lead (Evan Torres) to run repro on 2026-03-16.

title: Aurum Horizon Systems
preview:
Summary: Aurum Horizon operates route-optimization and real-time ETA services for large commercial fleets. They're evaluating Redwood Dedicated with multi-region reserved pools to guarantee throughput during peak routing windows and to meet contractual latency SLOs for enterprise customers.

Key customer quotes / snippets:
- 'We need predictable throughput for morning/evening load spikes — can't have degraded routing for 10k+ vessels.'
- 'Failover between us-east-1 and us-west-2 must be seamless; 15–20% headroom required.'

POC observations (short):
- Baseline (2-day): 120 reqs/s sustained on a mixed batch profile (avg payload 400 tokens); 95th pct latency for generation ~140ms using baseline A100 configs.
- Observed token distribution skewed: 60% short ( <128 ), 30% medium (128–512), 10% long (512–2048) — affects batching/windowing.

Commercial / procurement notes:
- Want reserved GPU pools across 2 regions (us-east, us-west) with Active-Passive failover; prefer mirrored capacity for EU pilot later.
- Target ARR band ~ $500k-$1M; 2-year commit preferred for pricing.

Internal ask from SE/AE:
- Need detailed per-AZ GPU counts and confirmation of headroom math for 99.95% availability target.
- Legal wants an explicit RTO for cross-AZ failover; procurement asked for credits table.

Stenography / misc:
- Drive links shared; awaiting answers to security matrix (drive:/security/aurum-security-questions.xlsx).
- Mentioned competitors evaluating: OpenAI enterprise endpoints and a self-hosted Kubernetes + Triton stack.

title: Oxley Threadlytics
preview:
Background: Oxley Threadlytics is a mid-market customer support platform used by ~300 customers (help centers, SMB to enterprise). They want to add an automated summarization + tagging layer to reduce agent handle time and improve routing. Primary integrations: Zendesk and Freshdesk via webhook/Apps. Key asks: high-fidelity 1-3 sentence summaries for long ticket threads, consistent auto-tags (product, urgency, channel), and macros/tool-calls to trigger refunds/NRR flows. They emphasized reliability during Black Friday-like spikes — need predictable autoscaling and graceful degradation (fallback to a smaller model or simple heuristics) rather than failures.

POC scope (agreed in call): 2-week pilot ingesting 5k anonymized tickets, enable real-time webhook path from Zendesk, run batch backfill for 90 days of tickets, evaluate: tag precision >= 85%, summary usefulness >= 4/5 by product team. SLO targets discussed: p95 latency for summary endpoint <= 300ms for short threads, throughput target 400-600 req/s during peak burst tests, tail resilience across 99.9th percentile.

Technical preferences: prefer VPC private integration, KMS-managed keys, audit logs forwarded to their SIEM. Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions around batching and quantization to hit cost targets. Would like ability to route high-value accounts to dedicated capacity and lower-cost fallback to hosted variants. They asked for example rollout plan that includes canary for 5% of agents and automatic fallback to rule-based summarizer if latency/SLA breach.

Security/process notes: Completed initial security questionnaire (attached). Next they need SOC2 attestation copy and confirm DPA language for EU data residency. Legal flagged retention controls and field-level redaction for PII as must-haves.

Quotes from last demo: "We need something that's predictable under load — last year our AI endpoint went to 10s of seconds during holiday and agents rolled back." — Head of Ops. "If tags are noisy we'll lose trust fast; precision is more important than recall for routing." — Support Director.

Internal Redwood fit notes: Good technical buyer (Head of Ops + Platform), champion in Support Director; procurement runway 2–3 months if POC success. Opportunity for expansion into Dedicated and Private for enterprise customers later.

title: Ventra AuditFlow Inc.
preview:
Initial enterprise inbound for Private VPC + per-tenant KMS. Heavy compliance focus (EEA residency + 7y retention for audit logs). Customer repeatedly: "We cannot export raw prompts outside EU or to third-party SaaS without DPA". Security team wants SIEM integration with Azure Sentinel (CEF or JSON over Event Hub), Splunk as secondary. Key ask: complete log schema and retention SLA for legal and infosec review.

Summary bullets:
- Procurement stage, commercial review started 2026-02.
- Security review kickoff 2026-02-17 (Fireflies ff_2026-02-17_ventra-demo).
- POC request: one-week private deployment in their EU VNet, dedicated HSM-backed KMS key, SIEM export enabled.
- They require request/response access logs (metadata only) + structured audit events for policy decisions.
- Redaction rule: remove full prompt text; store prompt_hash + sampling mechanism for QA (hashed + admin-access sample w/ consent).
- Customer wants token-level telemetry (counts, latency_ms) but not raw token text.
- Must provide mapping doc for their SIEM (CEF field names, sample events).
- Asked for retention choices: 90d / 1y / 7y; legal prefers 7y for suspicious activity investigations.

Quoted from CISO: "We need a deterministic schema we can ingest into Sentinel and forward to MSSP. No ambiguity on who can decrypt logs — per-tenant KMS only."

title: Cerulean Telecom Systems
preview:
Summary: Large North American telco evaluating Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) with air-gapped variant for NOC and central office deployments. Heavy emphasis on attestable supply chain (SBOM), FIPS/KMS integration, and streaming audit logs into Splunk.

Recent interactions: 
- 2026-03-02 call (ff_2026_0302_cerulean_call): demo of on-prem control plane; requested offline model upgrade workflow.
- 2026-02-19 security deep-dive: InfoSec requested proof-of-concepts for key management (HSM integration) and data flow diagrams. Gmail thread: thread-GM-cerulean-20260219-01.
- 2025-12-10 intro meeting with CTO and Head of Network Automation; showed latency numbers and dedicated GPU sizing.

Key requirements (from notes / quotes):
- "Cannot allow customer-PII or CPE telemetry to leave country boundary" => must support data residency and local-only inference.
- Air-gapped upgrades: signed model bundle delivery via SFTP/hard media; verified signatures on install.
- KMS/HSM: integrate with client on-prem HSM (Luna/AWS CloudHSM proxy) for envelope keys; support FIPS 140-2 validated modules.
- Audit logging: streaming token-level audit trail to Splunk, retention 7 years, immutable logs; SOC2/ISO path required.
- Network constraints: NOC has strict change windows (SLA for deploys); need deployment automation that supports maintenance windows and rollback.
- Performance: sub-30ms tail P99 for short-chat flows (local NOC inference), batch embedding throughput for nightly indexing (100k docs/hr).

POC scope discussed:
- On-prem control plane in customer VPC with private data plane inside their NOC.
- Air-gapped model update process: sign->hand-off->local-verify.
- KMS/HSM integration demo and attestation documents.
- Live audit log export to Splunk ingestion endpoint.

Open items / blockers: 
- InfoSec SRA outstanding: need SBOM, dependency vulnerability report, and installation checklist.
- Procurement: hardware lead time estimate required (GPU SKU and storage).
- Legal: finalize addendum for data residency and on-prem license terms.

Action items (AE/SE):
- Marcus to produce HSM integration runbook + sample config (due 2026-03-12) -> DONE (sent).
- Priya to coordinate procurement template with pricing (in progress).
- Send air-gapped deployment playbook + BOM (next_step).

Steno / CRM fragments: 
- "Client wants attestable SBOM + deterministic model bundles"
- "If Redwood can run in dark site NOC with the HSM integration we win"
- "Ask: can we support FIPS validated HSM modules in Private offering?"

References: drive://cerulean/security_faq_v2.pdf, drive://cerulean/vpc_onprem_install_runbook_v1.pptx, INFRA-4127, SEC-2089.

Contact list: 
- Technical buyer: Marie Kline, Head of Network Automation (marie.kline@cerulean-telecom.com)
- Procurement: Omar Reyes (omar.reyes@cerulean-telecom.com)
- InfoSec reviewer: Daniel Park (daniel.park@cerulean-telecom.com)

Likelihood / sentiment: High intent for private on-prem contract if InfoSec SRA closes; budget earmarked Q2 2026.

Short next notes to push: schedule 2-day onsite install window option; provide final BOM and signed model bundle procedure.

title: Lombard Harbor Research Vault
preview:
Summary: Lombard Harbor is a buy-side research platform (equities + fixed income) building a unified knowledge portal for analysts and desk apps. Primary need: large-scale RAG search with embeddings + reranking across proprietary research, regulatory filings, vendor datasets (~40M documents target index). Preference for Redwood Dedicated with VPC + customer KMS/HSM for key separation. Key asks: predictable latency for reranking (target 150-250ms end-to-end for top-k), embeddings throughput for nightly rebuilds (500k+ docs/day), and clear cost per query modeling (goal <$0.01 avg/query for common QA flows).

Security/Compliance: must support SSO/SAML, audit logging, per-tenant data residency (NA + EU), KMS with HSM-backed keys, retention controls and log export. Procurement/Legal: want SOC2/ISO evidence and a stronger data-residency SLA.

Quote from CTO on 2026-02-12 call: "We cannot accept co-mingled keys; need customer KMS and an SLA on regional data egress. Dedicated capacity is preferred for predictable performance."

POC notes: initial ingest + vectorization POC completed in Redwood sandbox (40M small docs simulated). Observed median rerank latency 220ms with current model pipeline; engineer asks for quantization / batching guidance to hit <200ms. Cost projection without optimization estimated ~ $0.015/query — too high. Customer asked for Optimize suggestions and Dedicated pool sizing (6-8 GPUs) estimate.

Preference for Redwood Dedicated with VPC + customer KMS/HSM for key separation. Key asks: predictable latency for reranking (target 150-250ms end-to-end for top-k), embeddings throughput for nightly rebuilds (500k+ docs/day), and clear cost per query modeling (goal <$0.01 avg/query for common QA flows).

Security/Compliance: must support SSO/SAML, audit logging, per-tenant data residency (NA + EU), KMS with HSM-backed keys, retention controls and log export. Procurement/Legal: want SOC2/ISO evidence and a stronger data-residency SLA.

Quote from CTO on 2026-02-12 call: "We cannot accept co-mingled keys; need customer KMS and an SLA on regional data egress. Dedicated capacity is preferred for predictable performance."

POC notes: initial ingest + vectorization POC completed in Redwood sandbox (40M small docs simulated). Observed median rerank latency 220ms with current model pipeline; engineer asks for quantization / batching guidance to hit <200ms. Cost projection without optimization estimated ~ $0.015/query — too high. Customer asked for Optimize suggestions and Dedicated pool sizing (6-8 GPUs) estimate.

title: CedarPoint Support Labs
preview: Goal: customer-support RAG system to deflect tickets and feed agent assist. Must index ~0.5M–4M historical tickets + KB, support incremental ingestion, and deliver top-5 retrieval + reranking within 200ms p95. Prefer a two-stage architecture: ANN coarse retriever (HNSW/IVF) + cross-encoder or lightweight transformer reranker for top-5 quality. Cost sensitivity: target <$0.005/query at current traffic; willing to pay for Dedicated for predictable latency. Security: VPC deployment, SSO, audit logs, KMS for keys, and retention controls. Must support SSO provisioning and produce an architecture diagram for infosec review.

title: HelioFin RegTech
preview:
Account summary (regulated fintech prospect)
- HelioFin building RegTech workflows for banks (case triage + investigation summaries + policy Q&A). They are evaluating Redwood primarily for Private (EU VPC) with a path to on-prem later.
- Strong interest in Shadow Traffic quality metrics during rollouts, but only if data handling is “extremely conservative.” Multiple stakeholders repeated: “we can’t create a new copy of regulated customer data that we can’t explain to auditors.”

Primary contacts (per call notes)
- VP Platform Engineering (technical buyer) + Head of Compliance Engineering (risk owner)
- Security officer joined last 20 min of 1/22 call; asked about prompt retention, access logging, and whether Redwood staff can see samples.

Use case / workload
- Mix of chat generation and retrieval-augmented Q&A (embeddings + reranking).
- Workloads include:
  - Investigation summaries (long context, structured output)
  - Policy Q&A (format adherence important)
  - Escalation triage (safety/refusal + instruction following)
- Model rollout pain today: they don’t have safe quality gates; current approach is “A/B in staging” which doesn’t match prod traffic distributions.

Shadow traffic requirements (must-haves for HelioFin)
- Default data minimization:
  - Redact PII-like fields and customer identifiers by default (allowlist preferred over regex-only).
  - Truncate long prompts/contexts beyond a configurable max.
  - Do not store raw prompts/responses unless explicitly enabled; if stored, must be in EU region and with explicit retention window.
- Retention controls:
  - Requested defaults: 7 days retention for shadow artifacts, configurable down to 24 hours.
  - Ability to disable example storage entirely while still computing aggregates.
  - Separate retention for (a) telemetry events (aggregates), (b) sampled examples, (c) scored outputs.
- Auditability:
  - Audit log entries for: enabling shadow policy, changing sampling %, adding targets, changing retention/redaction config, exporting examples.
  - Need “who/when/what changed” for internal compliance.
- Residency:
  - EU-only storage and processing for POC; no cross-region compute for scoring.
  - If using model-graded evals, they need clarity on whether grading model runs inside same EU boundary.
- Reliability / guardrails:
  - Hard requirement: fail-open (shadow path failures cannot impact user response).
  - Timeouts/circuit breakers must be documented; asked for explicit p99 budget and how shadow QPS is capped.

Product interest / packaging signals
- They’re comfortable paying for Dedicated/Private + add-on observability/retention controls.
- They asked if Shadow Traffic is an add-on and whether scoring compute is billed separately (they want predictable cost; concerned about “double inference” spend).

Current stage details
- In evaluation with security review in progress.
- They want a scoped POC:
  - One route, two variants (control + candidate model/quant profile)
  - Sampling initially 1% with a ramp plan
  - Metrics: format adherence, refusal/safety, rubric score for instruction following, plus latency/cost deltas

Recent activity timeline
- 2025-12-18: Inbound via partner intro (EU systems integrator). Initial qualification: Private deployment required, strict residency.
- 2026-01-07: Discovery call (Platform Eng). Key note: strong interest in safe rollouts; asked if we can “shadow new model without changing user output.”
- 2026-01-22: Technical deep dive (Fireflies: 2026-01-22-technical-deep-dive-shadow-evals-and-rollouts). Discussed shadow routing + scoring pipeline; HelioFin emphasized retention/redaction/audit requirements.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security FAQ + preliminary architecture diagram; HelioFin requested a written statement of default retention for shadow artifacts.
- 2026-02-05: Follow-up Q&A (security officer). Questions on who can access sampled examples, how exports are controlled, and whether audit logs cover policy changes.
- 2026-02-20: Internal Redwood sync (Priya + Marcus + Kevin Osei) to draft “Shadow Traffic Data Handling Addendum” for regulated customers.

Risks / concerns (internal)
- HelioFin may require on-prem or air-gapped scoring for model-graded evals in later phase; we should position v1 as EU VPC-only with clear constraints.
- They will likely reject any approach that stores raw prompts by default or lacks per-tenant retention overrides.
- They are sensitive to metric cardinality: asked whether we tag by customer_id/request_id (we should explicitly state we do not emit high-cardinality identifiers in metrics).

Open questions from HelioFin
- Can they bring their own redaction rules/allowlists (configurable patterns + structured field allowlist)?
- Can they export aggregates to their SIEM/warehouse without exporting examples?
- If scoring backlog occurs, do we pause shadowing automatically? How visible is scoring lag in the dashboard?

Next steps (owner)
- Priya to send a 1–2 page written addendum covering: default retention, redaction behavior, residency, and audit log events; include guardrails summary (timeouts, circuit breaker, fail-open).
- Marcus Lin to schedule joint security + architecture review; Kevin Osei to join for audit/SIEM questions.
- If approved, scope POC in EU VPC with 1% sampling and examples storage OFF by default (aggregates only).

title: Lumen Legaltech
preview:
Account summary
- Compliance-heavy (legal/regulatory). Building LLM features into internal case management + customer-facing contract review workflows.
- Strong preference for Dedicated capacity (predictable throughput + isolation). Private/VPC deployment likely required for production.

Firmographics / environment
- HQ: US (East). Primary users: enterprise legal ops + compliance teams.
- Current infra: AWS (us-east-1), strict network egress controls. Mentioned potential future EU footprint (data residency questions).

What they care about (high signal)
1) Auditability of scaling policy changes (hard requirement)
- They explicitly asked how we prove and reconstruct: who changed autoscaling config, when, what was changed (before/after), and which deployment(s) were affected.
- Quote (security review, 2025-07-22): “We need an immutable trail of any capacity or scaling change because it can materially impact user access and cost.”
- They want audit events not only for human-initiated updates (Console/API/Terraform), but also for autoscaler decisions (scale up/down) with reasoning.

2) Change management + RBAC
- Need role-based access controls for scaling policy updates (min/max bounds, predictive toggle, cooldowns).
- Asked whether we can restrict changes to a small admin group and whether reads can be broader (auditors).
- Asked about approval workflows; we do not have built-in approvals, but they can use internal change tickets + our audit logs as evidence.

3) Retention + export
- Retention request: 12 months minimum for audit logs; preferred 18–24 months.
- Export: they use Splunk and asked for either webhook streaming or periodic export (daily ok if guaranteed delivery + integrity).
- Also asked if we can provide a “config diff” view for audits.

4) Dedicated scaling bounds feature alignment
- They like the concept of customer-set min/max bounds as a spend control + baseline SLO guarantee.
- Concern: “If the platform changes behavior silently, we’ll fail audits.” They want explicit enablement, clearly documented semantics, and per-deployment effective policy visibility.

Workload details (current / planned)
- Use case: contract summarization + clause extraction, some chat-style assistant for internal users.
- Usage pattern: business hours heavy, some end-of-month spikes. Wants predictable p95 latency during peak.
- Model stance: open to Redwood-recommended models; more sensitive to compliance + audit trails than specific model brand.

Timeline of recent activity
- 2025-06-26: Inbound via partner referral; initial discovery (Dedicated vs Hosted).
- 2025-07-10: Technical deep dive on Dedicated capacity + VPC networking.
- 2025-07-22: Security review focused on audit logging + config change controls (Fireflies linked).
- 2025-08-05: Procurement call; MSA redlines returned; security appendix flagged audit logging and retention as required.
- 2025-08-07: Internal alignment with Platform team on upcoming autoscaling bounds + scaling decision audit events; created INT-9588 to track customer-facing answers + timeline.

Open questions to answer back to customer
- Exact audit event fields we can commit to for: scaling policy changes (min/max, predictive mode, cooldowns) and autoscaler decisions (requested desired vs bounded desired + reason).
- Retention guarantee by tier for enterprise Dedicated + whether export to SIEM is supported (or roadmap + interim solution).
- Clarify how “effective policy” is displayed (Console/API) and whether we can provide periodic evidence reports.

Internal notes / strategy
- This account is a strong fit for the autoscaling policy auditability workstream. If we can close the audit log + retention/export gap, likelihood to close is high.
- Coordinate responses with: Marcus Lin (security review), Kevin Osei (audit log export/SIEM), Felix Schneider (Console audit/scaling views).
- Avoid overpromising approvals workflow; emphasize deterministic audit trail + RBAC + integration to their existing change management.

Sentiment
- Positive on product performance and Dedicated isolation.
- Primary risk is compliance sign-off: they will not proceed without auditable scaling/config changes documented in writing.

title: Northpeak Security
preview:
Account summary
- Security-focused prospect building an internal-facing “analyst copilot” + customer-facing incident narrative generation. Current stack is mixed: some workloads on Bedrock, some DIY (vLLM) for cost control.
- Primary evaluation driver: auditability + predictable unit economics. Very sensitive to observability gaps.

What they care about (verbatim-ish)
- “We need to explain why the bill moved.” They want cache efficiency telemetry to be usable for internal chargeback by product area (route) and model version.
- “Audit logs can’t be an afterthought.” Needs clear event history for config changes, access, and deployment/rollout actions.

Product interest
- Redwood Private (VPC) is the lead option. Dedicated capacity is a backup if VPC timeline slips.
- Optimize add-on is attractive if recommendations are explainable and safe (no black-box auto-tuning).

Workload details
- Mix of chat + summarization; prompt sizes vary widely:
  - Short interactive chats for triage (hundreds of tokens)
  - Long incident bundles (multi-KB context; 8k–32k token windows depending on model)
- Traffic pattern: business-hours peaks; occasional burst during incident response.
- Latency target: p95 < 2.0s for “chat/triage” turns; summarization can be slower (p95 < 8–10s acceptable) if cost is low.

Evaluation requirements (must-have)
- Security/compliance
  - SSO/SAML (Okta), RBAC at least to workspace/project + operator roles.
  - Audit logging: access + config changes + model/version rollouts + key management events.
  - KMS integration required (AWS KMS). They asked whether customer-managed keys cover both data-at-rest and any cached artifacts.
  - Retention controls: ability to set retention window for logs/telemetry; they explicitly asked that raw prompts are not stored.
  - Data residency: US-only for most traffic; subset of Canadian customers may require CA region later (flagged as “phase 2” but procurement will ask).
- Observability / cost controls
  - Needs cache efficiency telemetry beyond a single hit-rate number:
    - Prefix cache hit-rate and KV reuse effectiveness
    - Miss reasons (esp. version mismatch and policy-disabled)
    - Eviction/pressure indicators
    - “Wasted prefill” (they called this out as a good way to show inefficiency to finance)
  - Must be sliceable by route + model + model version + region, but they are aware of cardinality risk; asked for how Redwood controls it.
  - Wants a monthly chargeback report: cost per team/route, with cache contribution (e.g., cost avoided / tokens saved from reuse).

Questions / concerns raised
- Auditability: asked if Redwood can prove when caching is enabled/disabled per route and whether that is captured in audit logs.
- Join keys: concerned about metric joins across gateway/runtime; asked for “one consistent route id” across logs and metrics.
- Streaming retries: they had a bad experience with “double counted tokens” elsewhere; asked how Redwood handles retries in metrics.
- Private deployment timeline: wants a realistic implementation plan (networking, KMS, logging sinks).

Recent activity timeline
- 2025-02-12: Inbound via security solutions page; initial qualification. Notes: evaluating Bedrock vs private inference vendor.
- 2025-02-19: Discovery call. Focus: VPC deployment, audit logs, retention. Marcus Lin joined to walk through security posture at a high level.
- 2025-03-05: Shared initial security packet + SOC 2 report under NDA (link withheld here; sent via secure portal).
- 2025-03-18: Technical deep dive (Fireflies: 2025-03-18-technical-deep-dive-cache-hit-rate-and-cost). They requested cache telemetry definitions + example dashboards; also requested a chargeback-friendly export format.
- 2025-03-20: Follow-up email thread: clarified that prompts should not be logged; telemetry must be privacy-safe (hashed/bucketed). Asked for a sample of miss-reason taxonomy and how we avoid cardinality blowups.

Internal notes / strategy
- This is a good fit for the cache telemetry sprint outcomes: they are explicitly asking for hit-rate + wasted prefill + miss reasons + eviction pressure, and for breakdowns by route/model/version.
- Position telemetry as: (1) operational guardrails for Redwood + (2) customer-facing cost accountability (chargeback) without storing prompts.
- Risk: they may over-index on per-tenant/per-user tagging. Be firm about allowlists/bucketing; offer route-level and org-level slices first.

Next steps (proposed)
- Send: cache telemetry one-pager (metric definitions + labels + sampling/cardinality policy summary) + sample dashboard screenshots.
- Set up: 60-min architecture review for VPC + KMS + audit log sinks (CloudWatch/S3) + retention controls.
- Confirm POC success criteria:
  - p95 latency targets met for triage route
  - Cache hit-rate + wasted prefill visible by route/model/version
  - Audit log events demonstrably cover rollout + caching config changes

References
- Fireflies: fireflies/2025-03-18-technical-deep-dive-cache-hit-rate-and-cost
- Related issue pattern (not their account, but relevant): SUP-3927 (dashboard showed zero hits due to label drops); use as internal caution to ensure ingestion/cardinality guards are configured before they see dashboards.

- Position telemetry as: (1) operational guardrails for Redwood + (2) customer-facing cost accountability (chargeback) without storing prompts.
- Risk: they may over-index on per-tenant/per-user tagging. Be firm about allowlists/bucketing; offer route-level and org-level slices first.

Next steps (proposed)
- Send: cache telemetry one-pager (metric definitions + labels + sampling/cardinality policy summary) + sample dashboard screenshots.
- Set up: 60-min architecture review for VPC + KMS + audit log sinks (CloudWatch/S3) + retention controls.
- Confirm POC success criteria:
  - p95 latency targets met for triage route
  - Cache hit-rate + wasted prefill visible by route/model/version
  - Audit log events demonstrably cover rollout + caching config changes

References
- Fireflies: fireflies/2025-03-18-technical-deep-dive-cache-hit-rate-and-cost
- Related issue pattern (not their account, but relevant): SUP-3927 (dashboard showed zero hits due to label drops); use as internal caution to ensure ingestion/cardinality guards are configured before they see dashboards.

title: Northbridge Healthcare
preview:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare system (mix of hospitals + outpatient). Evaluating Redwood primarily for Private deployment due to PHI handling + network controls.
- Current pain: internal platform team is stuck between (a) too-broad admin roles in existing tooling, and (b) brittle per-service IAM policies. They want consistent least-privilege, with strong audit trails for “who changed what” and “why access was denied.”

Workload / technical context
- Use cases under consideration:
  - Clinician-facing note summarization (high sensitivity; strict data residency + retention)
  - Internal knowledge base search over policies/procedures (embeddings + reranking)
  - Support agent assist for scheduling + billing (chat + tool calling)
- Performance: “latency matters but predictability matters more.” Target p95 for chat tokens < ~250ms/token streaming on internal network; embeddings throughput needs are bursty.
- Preference for running in their VPC / on-prem adjacent network. They flagged “no public internet egress from inference nodes” as a hard requirement.

RBAC / authorization requirements (main theme)
- Separation of duties is a hard requirement:
  - Security Admin: manage org/users/roles, audit log export/retention, key management integrations, break-glass approvals.
  - Deployment/Platform Operator: manage clusters, capacity, routing policies, model versions/pinning/rollouts within a constrained scope.
  - Auditor (read-only): view configs + audit logs, but cannot change anything.
- They explicitly asked if routing policy edits and model pinning can be scoped to an environment/cluster, not global.
- Quote from security lead: “Our platform admins cannot also be the people who grant themselves access.”
- They want deny-by-default, with explicit permissions for sensitive actions (API key rotation, secret changes, audit export, retention changes, rollback policies, etc.).

Scope model questions they asked (open items)
- How Redwood defines scope boundaries: org vs project vs environment vs cluster.
- Whether a role binding can be limited to “Prod only” (and not Dev/Test) for the same project.
- Whether the Console will show “why was I denied?” and if that can be exposed only to admins.

Audit logging / evidence needs
- Wants audit log export to SIEM (Splunk). Specifically requested:
  - Include authz decision context: subject, action, scope, policy version, allow/deny, and denial reason.
  - Deny events should be logged (at least for admin/control-plane actions).
  - Time sync + immutable storage guidance for evidence.
- They asked for sample audit events to attach to internal security review docs.

Identity requirements
- SSO: SAML required (Okta). They also asked about SCIM (nice-to-have) but not a blocker for POC.
- Group mapping: must support IdP group -> Redwood role template assignment (esp. for Private bootstrap).

Private deployment / operational requirements
- Bootstrap: want a clearly documented first-admin flow. They asked what happens if the only admin leaves the org or loses IdP access.
- Break-glass: requested written procedure (who approves, time-bound elevation, alerting). They want alerts to their SecOps distribution list + Redwood SecOps during POC.

Commercial / process notes
- Procurement will require security questionnaire + architecture diagram + SOC 2 report (already have Redwood SOC 2 attestation in progress per their ask).
- They are open to a short POC if RBAC v2 + audit export are “real and enforceable,” not roadmap-only.

Internal Redwood notes
- Marcus Lin to provide:
  - RBAC v2 permission catalog excerpt relevant to Private admin workflows
  - Example audit log entries (allow/deny) including authz fields
- Colin to coordinate next workshop and bring Vivek (Private deployments) if installer/bootstrap questions continue.

Risks
- If RBAC scope model lands as org-only for key operational actions, they indicated they will fall back to self-hosting (vLLM) with custom gates.
- They are sensitive to any authz caching that could cause stale permissions (“we had an incident with stale group claims last year”).

Next customer-facing deliverables requested
- 1-pager: recommended role templates for healthcare orgs (Security Admin / Deployment Operator / Auditor) + what each can/can’t do.
- Sample SIEM export payload mapping (Splunk-friendly fields).
- Written break-glass flow for Private deployments (time-bound, approvals, post-use review).

title: Nordic Bank
preview:
Account summary
- Large EU-headquartered bank. Exploring LLM inference vendor for internal customer support + knowledge workflows.
- Primary evaluation driver: EU data residency + strong auditability + ability to report/attribute costs by region/business unit.
- They are skeptical of “black box” managed APIs; want clarity on where data flows and how logs are handled.

Current status (as of 2025-02-22)
- Stage: Evaluation (security review in progress, solution architecture being validated).
- Commercial: early pricing alignment pending (Dedicated EU capacity vs Private VPC deployment).

Key contacts (customer)
- Head of AI Platform (technical sponsor)
- Security Architecture lead (controls, audit logging, SIEM)
- Data Protection Officer team (DPIA/DPA review)
- Finance/Procurement (wants region-specific unit economics; chargeback model)

Use cases / workloads
- Support agent assist (chat + tool calling): internal contact center; expects heavy peak hours.
- Document search + summarization (embeddings + rerank): policy docs, product terms, incident retros.
- Internal email/meeting summarization (batch jobs); strict retention rules.

Technical requirements
- Residency: “EU-only means EU-only” for inference traffic, persistent storage, and observability data. They asked explicitly about:
  - Where request/response logs live
  - Where token metering and cost telemetry are stored
  - Whether support engineers can access payloads (answer: should be no; need redaction + role-based access)
- Network: prefer Private (VPC) with customer-managed networking. Open to Dedicated in EU if control plane + logs can be constrained.
- Identity: SSO/SAML required; SCIM preferred but not a hard requirement for pilot.
- Audit: exportable audit logs (user access, config changes, key usage) to their SIEM.
- Data retention: default “no prompt/response retention”; any operational logs must be configurable with explicit TTL.
- Key management: KMS integration; asked about HSM roadmap (not required for pilot).

Cost / reporting requirements (strong emphasis)
- Need cost visibility by region (EU subregions) + model + deployment tier.
- Finance asked for something like “blended $/1M output tokens by region” so they can allocate spend across business units and compare vendors.
- Quote from security review: “We can’t approve something if we can’t audit it and we can’t allocate it.”
- They asked how we handle idle capacity in Dedicated and how that shows up in cost reporting (i.e., blended vs marginal).
- We referenced internal work on the Unit Economics dashboard (internal-only today) and discussed what we can provide in QBR-style reporting while external dashboards are not productized.

Security review notes (from Fireflies)
- Meeting: 2025-02-14-security-and-data-retention-review-nordicbank
- Topics covered:
  - Data flow: gateway -> runtime -> metering -> warehouse (they want diagram + list of subprocessors)
  - Logs: confirm prompt/response not stored by default; confirm any debugging logs are gated and redacted
  - Retention: configurable TTL; policy must be enforceable; need to document defaults
  - Audit logs: need export mechanism + event types list
  - Incident response: they asked for notification timelines; also asked about past incidents (answer: provide summary + policy)

Recent activity timeline
- 2025-01-29: intro + requirements capture (EU-only + audit/retention called out immediately).
- 2025-02-06: technical deep dive on deployment options (Dedicated EU vs Private VPC).
- 2025-02-14: security & data retention review (Fireflies logged). Action item: provide DPIA starter pack + subprocessor list.
- 2025-02-18: finance follow-up: asked for region-specific cost breakdown and methodology (rate card provenance, idle allocation).
- 2025-02-21: internal: looped in Marcus Lin (security review) + Sean Gallagher (SRE) for SLOs/operational posture; discussed what reporting we can commit to for pilot.

Open questions
- Which EU regions are acceptable for compute + telemetry storage (e.g., eu-north vs eu-west)? They may restrict to specific countries.
- Do they require on-prem in later phase? (They said “not now,” but asked about long-term option.)
- For cost visibility: do they want per-tenant breakdown or just aggregated by business unit? (We must avoid exposing customer-identifiable info in any shared reports.)

Next steps (internal)
- Provide: EU-only architecture diagram + data flow, retention defaults, audit log event catalog.
- Share: cost methodology overview (how $/1M output tokens is computed and reconciled) + note on rate card versioning.
- Schedule: working session with their security architecture team to map controls to our features (RBAC, audit logs, retention, KMS).
- Prep: pilot proposal (success criteria: latency targets, residency validation, audit log export test, cost reporting sample by region/model).

Risks / deal notes
- If we cannot convincingly scope EU-only telemetry + support access controls, risk of security rejection.
- Competitors are pushing “EU region endpoints” but may not satisfy their audit/retention requirements either; we should lean into our Private + observability controls.
- They will likely use region-specific unit economics as a gating criterion during procurement (not just performance).

title: LucidQuanta Realtime Economics
preview:
Summary: mid-market quant trading platform; heavy concentration on sub-40ms p50 inference for chat assistants and sub-10ms reranking paths. Extremely cost-sensitive at scale — want predictable per-token economics with reserved capacity. Interested primarily in Redwood Dedicated + Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization) but needs private VPC and KMS integration for regulatory reasons.

Requirements (from SE call):
- Workloads: real-time chat (avg prompt+context 120 tokens), streaming responses, reranking microservice (short inputs, high QPS), embeddings for document search (background batch).
- Targets: chat p50 <= 40ms, chat p95 <= 120ms; reranking tail < 15ms; embeddings throughput 10k qps batch window.
- Cost: try to keep token unit economics < 0.0008 USD/token effective (after batching/kv cache).
- Model prefs: openquant-7b/8bit quant variants for low-latency production; fallback to openquant-13b for complex queries with graceful routing.
- Routing needs: region affinity (NA primary, failover EU), auto-fallback to cheaper quantized variant when Dedicated capacity saturated, prefer cold-start warmup to avoid tail spikes.
- Storage/retention: 30d request logs, audit trail required for model outputs, must support SSO & SCIM provisioning.

What we proposed:
- 2-week POC: run representative reranking kube job + chat stream; measure p50/p95 on dedicated pool (NVIDIA A40 equiv) with continuous batching and KV prefix caching enabled. Include variant with 8-bit quant and perf/quality delta.
- Redwood Optimize runbook: recommended batch sizes, cache TTLs, quantization knobs (int8 + tuned kernels), throughput vs latency curves.
- Security: provide KMS integration notes + SOC2 docs; SE shared sample IAM & VPC peering template (drive link).

Customer feedback snippets:
- "If we can hold <40ms p50 reliably at 2k qps, we can roll to production." — VP Eng
- "Need explicit cost sims for peak vs steady-state; finance won't sign without ARR sensitivity." — Head of Ops

Open items / blockers:
- Internal legal requires HSM/KMS attestation before POC.
- Network team needs egress rules for VPC peering; may add 2 weeks.
- Want perf benchmark harness (JMeter + custom trace shim) from Redwood to reproduce runs.

Next steps (short):
- Marcus to schedule POC kickoff (env details) — target week of 2025-03-03.

title: Helmcrest RetainWorks
preview:
Account summary: mid-market retention SaaS focused on lifecycle messaging and churn prediction. Primary product 'RetainBot' — customer success automation + playbooks. Interested in Redwood because of predictable unit economics and plan to move from Hosted API (fast onboarding) to Dedicated capacity for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Use-case & acceptance criteria:
- Embeddings-based semantic search over customer help docs and transcripts (1M resources) — target recall > 0.88 and 95th pct latency < 400ms.
- Reranking layer for candidate responses (rerank top 25) to improve NDCG for support suggestions.
- Chat assistant for agents: latency budget 200-300ms p50, cost target to be < $0.10 per 1k active users/month token costs.

POC scope & metrics:
- Phase 1 (Hosted API): validate model quality vs baseline (OpenAI gpt-4-mini) on in-house eval set; measure tokens/response, p50/p95 latency, and embedding recall.
- Phase 2 (Dedicated POC): run 3-day soak with 2x traffic peak, test autoscale/burst, measure tail latency, and reproduce cost numbers with batching/caching and quantized model variant.
- Success criteria: quality delta <= 3% vs baseline; 30-45% reduction in token cost via batching + prefix caching + quantized model without >5% drop in eval metrics.

Cost optimization workshop highlights (2/5):
- Batching: suggested dynamic batching with 30-60ms max addt'l latency for 4-6x throughput gains on smaller prompts. Presented sample rerouter config.
- Caching: prefix & KV cache idea for repeat customer contexts — expected to cut tokens by ~18-25% for repeat agent workflows.
- Quantization: SE recommended int8 + selective fp16 for critical layers. Need to validate model accuracy on eval set — engineering flagged a 2.8% drop for candidate LLM on NDCG in dry run.
- Unit economics: finance asked for a 12-month TCO model comparing Hosted pay-as-you-go vs Dedicated reserved capacity w/ 20% commit. Early model shows Dedicated favorable at >= 500k tokens/day workload.

Security/compliance notes:
- Must support SSO (SAML) for agent UI.
- Data residency request: logs must be stored in US-only region for now.
- Require KMS integration for key management and audit log retention for 1 year.
- Security team wants SOC2 + running incident response playbook; Redwood provided SOC2 doc link in drive.

Quotes from stakeholders:
- Head of Product (Maya Li): "We need predictable pricing — cost surprises kill PLG expansion. If you can show 40% cost savings in Dedicated POC we're likely to move forward."
- Eng Manager (Diego Ramos): "Latency tail is our blocker — can Redwood meet p95 under load with batching? Also want to test quantized throughput on our test harness."

Open technical questions:
- Which quantization profile gives best latency/cost tradeoff without >3% quality drop? (SE to run int8+fp16 hybrid tests).
- How to route heavy cognitive workloads to Dedicated while keeping low-volume bursts on Hosted to save commit? (Asked for routing policy doc).
- Exact SLA numbers for Dedicated p95 under 99th percentile traffic — Redwood asked for sample traffic trace (provided).

Next steps (short):
- Aisha to deliver cost-optimization deck with estimated unit economics by 2025-02-15.
- Jordan to coordinate finance call w/ Helmcrest on 2025-02-18 to discuss commit sizing.
- SE to run quantization accuracy sweep and share results (Jira INF-8590).
- Security: Redwood to provide detailed KMS integration steps and updated SOC2 artifact link (drive://security/helmcrest-scan-answers.pdf).

Linked artifacts & tickets: see drive doc for pricing deck; fireflies for demo and workshop transcripts; INF-8421 (latency regression investigation), INF-8590 (quantization accuracy job).

Short CRM shorthand items: "POC live - metrics gathering", "workshop valuable — product liked batching/caching examples", "finance cares about commit math", "security still needs answers (KMS/SOC2)"

- Quantization: SE recommended int8 + selective fp16 for critical layers. Need to validate model accuracy on eval set — engineering flagged a 2.8% drop for candidate LLM on NDCG in dry run.
- Unit economics: finance asked for a 12-month TCO model comparing Hosted pay-as-you-go vs Dedicated reserved capacity w/ 20% commit. Early model shows Dedicated favorable at >= 500k tokens/day workload.

Security/compliance notes:
- Must support SSO (SAML) for agent UI.
- Data residency request: logs must be stored in US-only region for now.
- Require KMS integration for key management and audit log retention for 1 year.
- Security team wants SOC2 + running incident response playbook; Redwood provided SOC2 doc link in drive.

Quotes from stakeholders:
- Head of Product (Maya Li): "We need predictable pricing — cost surprises kill PLG expansion. If you can show 40% cost savings in Dedicated POC we're likely to move forward."
- Eng Manager (Diego Ramos): "Latency tail is our blocker — can Redwood meet p95 under load with batching? Also want to test quantized throughput on our test harness."

Open technical questions:
- Which quantization profile gives best latency/cost tradeoff without >3% quality drop? (SE to run int8+fp16 hybrid tests).
- How to route heavy cognitive workloads to Dedicated while keeping low-volume bursts on Hosted to save commit? (Asked for routing policy doc).
- Exact SLA numbers for Dedicated p95 under 99th percentile traffic — Redwood asked for sample traffic trace (provided).
